---
title: "Module 2: Data Structures"
---

## Module Overview

In this module, you'll learn about Python's built-in data structures, which allow you to organize and store collections of data efficiently.

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this module, you will be able to:

- Create and manipulate lists and tuples
- Use list methods and understand list indexing and slicing
- Work with dictionaries to store key-value pairs
- Use sets for unique collections of items
- Perform advanced string operations and formatting
- Choose the appropriate data structure for different scenarios

## Module Contents

### Lesson 1: Lists and Tuples
Learn about ordered collections of items and how to manipulate them.

### Lesson 2: Dictionaries and Sets
Explore key-value pairs and unique collections of elements.

### Lesson 3: String Operations
Master advanced string manipulation techniques.

## Prerequisites

You should have completed Module 1 and be comfortable with:
- Variables and data types
- Basic operations
- Input and output

::: {.callout-tip}
## Data Structures in Math

Data structures are especially useful in mathematical computing for:
- Storing sequences of numbers
- Representing matrices and vectors
- Organizing datasets
- Mapping relationships between elements
:::

## Estimated Time

This module should take approximately 5-7 hours to complete, including exercises and practice time.

## Ready to Start?

Begin with [Lesson 1: Lists and Tuples](lesson1.qmd)!
